questions.questionText: If $\lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 1^{+}} \frac{(x-1)(6+\lambda \cos (x-1))+\mu \sin (1-x)}{(x-1)^3}=-1$, where $\lambda, \mu \in \mathbb{R}$, then $\lambda+\mu$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be a continuous function satisfying $f(0)=1$ and $f(2 x)-f(x)=x$ for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$. If $\lim _\limits{n \rightarrow \infty}\left\{f(x)-f\left(\frac{x}{2^n}\right)\right\}=G(x)$, then $\sum_\limits{r=1}^{10} G\left(r^2\right)$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $m$ and $n$ be the number of points at which the function $f(x)=\max \left\{x, x^3, x^5, \ldots x^{21}\right\}, x \in \mathbb{R}$, is not differentiable and not continuous, respectively. Then $m+n$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:

$$f(x)=|x-1| \cos |x-2| \sin |x-1|+(x-3)\left|x^{2}-5 x+4\right|$$, is NOT differentiable, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\alpha \mathrm{e}^{x}+\beta \mathrm{e}^{-x}+\gamma \sin x}{x \sin ^{2} x}=\frac{2}{3}$$, where $$\alpha, \beta, \gamma \in \mathbf{R}$$, then which of the following is NOT correct?
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let [t] denote the greatest integer less than or equal to t. Let
f(x) = x $$-$$ [x], g(x) = 1 $$-$$ x + [x], and h(x) = min{f(x), g(x)}, x $$\in$$ [$$-$$2, 2]. Then h is :
options:
(A) continuous in [$$-$$2, 2] but not differentiable at more than
four points in ($$-$$2, 2)
(B) not continuous at exactly three points in [$$-$$2, 2]

(C) continuous in [$$-$$2, 2] but not differentiable at exactly
three points in ($$-$$2, 2)
(D) not continuous at exactly four points in [$$-$$2, 2]
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Consider the function $$f:(0, \infty) \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$$ defined by $$f(x)=e^{-\left|\log _e x\right|}$$. If $$m$$ and $$n$$ be respectively the number of points at which $$f$$ is not continuous and $$f$$ is not differentiable, then $$m+n$$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $f, g$ and $h$ be the real valued functions defined on $\mathbb{R}$ as

$f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}\frac{x}{|x|}, & x \neq 0 \\ 1, & x=0\end{array}\right.$

$g(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}\frac{\sin (x+1)}{(x+1)}, & x \neq-1 \\ 1, & x=-1\end{array}\right.$

and $h(x)=2[x]-f(x)$, where $[x]$ is the greatest integer $\leq x$. Then the

value of $\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 1} g(h(x-1))$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The value of

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} \left( {{x \over {\root 8 \of {1 - \sin x} - \root 8 \of {1 + \sin x} }}} \right)$$ is equal to :

questions.questionText:
If $$\alpha $$ is positive root of the equation, p(x) = x2 - x - 2 = 0, then

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {\alpha ^ + }} {{\sqrt {1 - \cos \left( {p\left( x \right)} \right)} } \over {x + \alpha - 4}}$$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: Let $$f(x) = x.\left[ {{x \over 2}} \right]$$,  for -10< x < 10, where [t] denotes the greatest integer function. Then the number of points of discontinuity of f is equal to _____.

questions.questionText: Let [x] be the greatest integer less than or equals to x. Then, at which of the following point(s) the function $$f(x) = x\cos (\pi (x + [x]))$$ is discontinuous?

questions.questionText: Let f : R $$ \to $$ (0, 1) be a continuous function. Then, which of the following function(s) has (have) the value zero at some point in the interval (0, 1) ?

If $$g(x) = \int\limits_x^{\pi /2} {[f'(t)\text{cosec}\,t - \cot t\,\text{cosec}\,t\,f(t)]dt} $$

for $$x \in \left( {0,\,{\pi \over 2}} \right]$$, then $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} g(x)$$ =
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $[t]$ denote the greatest integer less than or equal to $t$. If the function

$$ f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cl} b^2 \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}\left[\frac{\pi}{2}(\cos x+\sin x) \cos x\right]\right), & x<0 \\ \frac{\sin x-\frac{1}{2} \sin 2 x}{x^3} & , x>0 \\ a & , x=0 \end{array}\right. $$

is continuous at $x=0$, then $a^2+b^2$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: Let $y=y(x)$ be a differentiable function in the interval $(0, \infty)$ such that $y(1)=2$, and $\lim\limits_{t \rightarrow x}\left(\frac{t^2 y(x)-x^2 y(t)}{x-t}\right)=3$ for each $x > 0$. Then $2 y(2)$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $$f:(0, \pi) \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$$ be a function given by $$f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}\left(\frac{8}{7}\right)^{\frac{\tan 8 x}{\tan 7 x}}, & 0< x<\frac{\pi}{2} \\ \mathrm{a}-8, & x=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ (1+\mid \cot x)^{\frac{\mathrm{b}}{\mathrm{a}}|\tan x|}, & \frac{\pi}{2} < x < \pi\end{array}\right.$$

where $$\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b} \in \mathbf{Z}$$. If $$f$$ is continuous at $$x=\frac{\pi}{2}$$, then $$\mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2$$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{\mathrm{a} x^2+2 \mathrm{a} x+3}{4 x^2+4 x-3} & , x \neq-\frac{3}{2}, \frac{1}{2} \\ \mathrm{~b} & , x=-\frac{3}{2}, \frac{1}{2}\end{cases}$ be continuous at $x=-\frac{3}{2}$. If $f \circ f(x)=\frac{7}{5}$, then $x$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If f : R $$ \to $$ R is a function defined by f(x)= [x - 1] $$\cos \left( {{{2x - 1} \over 2}} \right)\pi $$, where [.] denotes the greatest
integer function, then f is :
options: (B) discontinuous at all integral values of x except at x = 1
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $\mathbb{R}$ denote the set of all real numbers. Define the function $f : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ by

$f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}2-2 x^2-x^2 \sin \frac{1}{x} & \text { if } x \neq 0, \\ 2 & \text { if } x=0 .\end{array}\right.$

Then which one of the following statements is TRUE?
options:
(B)

There is a positive real number $\delta$, such that $f$ is a decreasing function on the interval $(0, \delta)$

(C)

For any positive real number $\delta$, the function $f$ is NOT an increasing function on the interval $(-\delta, 0)$

(D)

$x = 0$ is a point of local minima of $f$
questions.subject: Mathematics
